title: Random Forest
description: Combine many decision trees to build a stronger random forest model.
takeaways: A random forest combines many decision trees into one ensemble model.
Voting across trees often produces more stable predictions.
Random forests are a strong baseline for tabular classification problems.

practiceLab.objective: Create a tiny RandomForestClassifier, fit it on four points, and predict the label for [1, 1].
instructions: Run the import cell to create RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=10, random_state=42) and confirm Random forest ready! prints.
Run the training-data cell to define X and y, fit the model, and confirm Model trained! prints.
Run the prediction cell for input [[1, 1]].
Optional: predict [[0, 0]] in a new line, re-run, and compare the two predictions.
Optional: raise n_estimators to 50, re-fit, and check whether the [[1, 1]] prediction stays the same.
hints: Keep random_state=42 for reproducible fits on this tiny dataset.
model.predict expects a 2D array-like such as [[1, 1]].
n_estimators controls how many trees vote in the forest.
practiceLab.expectedOutput: Printed readiness and training confirmations, then Prediction: followed by the predicted class for [1, 1] (0 or 1).
